title: Creamy Aerated Argentinian Mincemeat Empanadas
description: A unique and flavorful post-workout empanada dish, made with aerated beurre-monte, jain-friendly mincemeat, and a touch of argentinian flair.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
In a large bowl, combine the flour, salt, and cold butter. Mix until the butter is evenly distributed and the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add water to the flour mixture and mix until a dough forms. Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a ball and flatten it slightly into a disk.
Place a tablespoon of mincemeat in the center of each disk, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Fold the dough in half over the mincemeat, pressing the edges together to seal the empanada. Brush the tops with olive oil.
Bake the empanadas for 20-25 minutes, or until they are golden brown.

While the empanadas are baking, prepare the beurre-monte. In a small bowl, whip the heavy cream until it becomes stiff. Add the softened butter and continue whipping until the mixture is smooth and aerated.
Once the empanadas are done, let them cool for a few minutes before serving. Spoon the beurre-monte over the top of each empanada and garnish with chopped parsley.
